_meta.description: Single source of truth for values that BOTH the ECHO Coaching Manual AND the Foundations Manual reference. Foundations imports this file at build time via scripts/sync-canonical.mjs. Never duplicate these values in Foundations source — always reference them by key.

zones._doc: Heart-rate zones expressed as percentages of LTHR (lactate threshold heart rate). These are the foundational HR zones — Foundations references them via {{canonical.zones.zN.range}}. Power and pace zones are NOT in canonical because they are ECHO-internal.

block_types._doc: Foundational block-type vocabulary. The full ECHO Manual adds more advanced block variants — Foundations only references these intro-level ones.

base.name: Base
base.intent: Build aerobic capacity and durability. Mostly Z1-Z2 volume.

build.name: Build
build.intent: Introduce threshold and tempo work on top of aerobic base. Z3-Z4 emphasis.

peak.name: Peak
peak.intent: Race-specific intensity and durability. Mix of Z3-Z5 with race-pace work.

taper.name: Taper
taper.intent: Reduce volume while preserving sharpness ahead of A-race.

recovery.name: Recovery
recovery.intent: Active recovery week, ~50-65% normal volume, Z1-Z2 only.

testing._doc: Foundational testing terminology. ECHO Manual contains the formula-level detail — Foundations only references the names/concepts.

lthr.name: LTHR (Lactate Threshold Heart Rate)
lthr.summary: The HR you can sustain for ~1 hour of all-out effort. Anchor for HR zones.

rpe.name: RPE (Rate of Perceived Exertion)
rpe.summary: 10-point subjective effort scale, used alongside HR/power/pace for athlete feedback.

terminology._doc: Words/phrases that must be used CONSISTENTLY between ECHO and Foundations. If you change one of these in ECHO, the drift-check will flag Foundations.
